message:
fix(dag): transitive_closure handles multigraph + cyclic + undirected + reflexive (br-tcmg)

Round 23 Cycle 2 — multigraph audit:

[HIGH] br-tcmg: fnx.transitive_closure had three real parity bugs:
(1) MultiDiGraph input downgraded to DiGraph, losing parallel-edge
    preservation nx provides;
(2) cyclic DiGraph omitted self-loops (u,u) for nodes that can reach
    themselves via a path of length >= 1 (e.g. every node in a
    3-cycle should get a self-loop in the non-reflexive TC);
(3) undirected input raised NetworkXNotImplemented while nx supports
    it.

Fixed by delegating to nx for multigraphs, undirected, cyclic-directed,
and reflexive=True cases. Rust fast-path retained for acyclic
DiGraphs where none of the three issues apply — the common TC case.

Other multigraph algorithm probes (density, is_connected/tree/forest,
shortest_path+weight, dijkstra_path_length, pagerank, clustering,
number_of_selfloops, wiener_index, transitivity, in/out_degree,
is_strongly_connected, simple_cycles, strongly_connected_components,
hits, topological_sort/transitive_closure on cycle-free DAG) — all
match nx exactly.

Full suite: 4914 passed, 18 skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5560,7 +5560,30 @@ def _union_with_rename_via_parity(G, H, rename):
 
 
 def transitive_closure(G, reflexive=False):
-    """br-isokw: ``G`` matches nx; Rust binding used ``g``."""
+    """Return the transitive closure of ``G``.
+
+    br-isokw: ``G`` matches nx.
+
+    br-tcmg: nx.transitive_closure on a MultiDiGraph preserves the
+    MultiDiGraph class and includes self-loops ``(u, u)`` for any
+    node ``u`` that participates in a cycle (i.e. can reach itself
+    via a path of length >= 1 when reflexive=False). fnx's Rust
+    binding returned a plain DiGraph with no cycle self-loops.
+    Delegate for multigraphs and for graphs known to contain cycles
+    (where the self-loop distinction matters).
+    """
+    # Delegate for: multigraphs (class preservation), undirected
+    # (Rust binding doesn't accept them), cyclic directed (cycle
+    # self-loops), and reflexive=True (uncommon).
+    if (
+        G.is_multigraph()
+        or not G.is_directed()
+        or reflexive
+        or (G.is_directed() and not is_directed_acyclic_graph(G))
+    ):
+        return _call_networkx_for_parity(
+            "transitive_closure", G, reflexive=reflexive
+        )
     return _raw_transitive_closure(G, reflexive=reflexive)
